The site

Home Architecture Code Formal Verification The Lab Meta Tools Engine Room Systems Atlas Sims Gallery Videos

Systems showcase · deep dive

Instruments, not illustrations

Nothing on this page is a picture of physics. Drag a charge and the field lines are re-traced by the same integrator that draws the diagrams in the worksheets. Press Run the simulation and the engine solves the motion, live, in your browser. No movies, no recordings, no requests off this page.

Every figure in a highlighted box is read from the curated scene or the repository, with a source and a verified date — hover to read them. Of 104 registered scenarios, 39 weld their physics to an independent check.

Drag it

The Electric Field Lab

Drag either charge. The electric field lines re-trace as you move it — this is not a picture, it is the same field-line integrator the platform uses to draw its diagrams, running in your browser. Select a charge and use the ← → ↑ ↓ keys to nudge it; Tab switches which charge is focused. Drag the two together and the lines stay well-behaved — a minimum-separation clamp keeps the field finite.

Idle until you touch it — nothing animates on its own.

Drag a charge, tap it to set its strength, or click empty space to add another. Select a charge and press Delete to remove an added one; the + / − keys change its charge.

How the live math is pinned to the library

The tracer in assets/fieldlab/field_tracer.js is a port of the platform’s own reference implementation — lib/charges.py’s compute_field_line, the same fixed-step integration along the unit field direction. A build-time check (tools/test_field_tracer.py) drives this browser tracer from the parameters recorded in a charges.py-generated fixture and asserts the polylines match within tolerance, so the interactive exhibit cannot silently drift from the library it demonstrates. When it last ran, the two agreed to the last floating-point bit.

Prefer no motion? The field lab idles until you touch it, each simulation waits for an explicit Run, and the embedded simulator honors reduced-motion — it loads the scene and holds rather than autoplaying. With JavaScript off, the poster still and this caption remain.

The engine’s physics, running live. The field lab traces with the library’s own integrator; each simulation boots the real simulator in a same-origin frame — sourced and dated, with no external request. Nothing here is a recording.
